data.title: Toro Corp. reports net income of $1.3M for Q3 and $4.3M for 9 months ended Sept. 30, 2025. Energy transportation provider.
data.description: On December 5, 2025, in Limassol, Cyprus, Toro Corp. (NASDAQ: TORO), a leading global energy transportation company, disclosed its financial outcomes for the periods concluding on September 30, 2025. The report covered the company's performance over both the three-month and nine-month stretches.

data.title: NYSE partners with LA-based podcast TBPN, provides daily pre-market update. Details from December 5th given by Ashley Mastronardi.
data.description: On December 5, 2025, the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) released its daily pre-market update through PRNewswire. This report offers market insights directly from the NYSE Trading Floor to assist traders ahead of the opening bell. Ashley Mastronardi presented the pre-market update for the day, providing essential information for investors. Stay informed with the latest data from the NYSE to make informed trading decisions.

data.title: Eucatex, led by CEO Flavio Maluf, wins "Best Product – Real Estate Line" at Abrart 2025 Award in São Paulo.
data.description: SÃO PAULO--Eucatex, headed by CEO Flavio Maluf, secured the "Best Product – Real Estate Line" award at the Abrart 2025 Award ceremony on November 6, 2025, at the Círculo Militar Club in São Paulo. The event assembled individuals from the paint sector including retailers, manufacturers, and distributors. Acknowledged by the Brazilian Association of Paint Retailers (Abrart), this award honors outstanding companies in specialized retail. CEO Maluf led Eucatex to be recognized among leading industry peers, emphasizing their commitment to excellence in the real estate product line.

data.title: WADL-TV in Detroit, a Top 14 TV market, removes Lionsgate's MovieSphere Gold from its lineup. WADL built from a permit 35 years ago.
data.description: In Detroit, Michigan, WADL, a TV station serving a Top 14 market, has recently decided to eliminate Lionsgate's MovieSphere Gold from its channel lineup. This change comes as WADL marks its 35th year since being established under a construction permit. WADL is known for being the last remaining...

data.title: National Police Association endorses the Strengthening Resources for Our Schools Act, supporting school resource officers for safer schools.
data.description: In Indianapolis on December 5, 2025, it was highlighted that school resource officers (SROs) play a vital role in ensuring school safety. These officers are seen as key in addressing the prevalent issue of violence in schools across the nation. Their ability to swiftly respond to active shooter situations and relay important information to other officers on the scene makes them crucial assets in maintaining security.

data.title: Morphy's Dec. 10-11 Auction to showcase luxurious items like 48-carat necklace, Rolex watches, Tiffany lamps, and unique art.
data.description: In the spotlight at Morphy's annual Winter Fine & Decorative auction in Denver, PA, on December 5, 2025, was an exquisite collection of luxury items. The auction showcased a stunning 48-carat platinum and diamond necklace, along with ruby and diamond earrings. Among the premium wristwatches up for bidding were coveted brands like Rolex and Cartier. The event also featured elegant Tiffany lamps, a rare Amphora dragon vase, and a 14K gold Tiffany jewel box once owned by a renowned movie star. This exclusive auction presented high-end items that appealed to collectors and enthusiasts alike.
Congresswoman Biggs has revealed recent stock transactions.

data.title: Waterdrop's revenue surged by 38.4% in Q3, with new tech services segment contributing 23% of revenue, despite lackluster stock performance.
data.description: Waterdrop Inc., an insurance broker, saw a 38.4% increase in revenue in the third quarter, with its young technical services business experiencing a 20-fold rise. The company is expected to return to strong revenue growth this year after three years of declines. The new technical services segment went from nearly no revenue last year to contributing 23% of revenue in the third quarter. Despite impressive growth, the company's shares only slightly rose after the announcement. The insurance brokerage business, which makes up the majority of Waterdrop's revenue, is facing challenges due to new regulations in China limiting profits. In response, Waterdrop is focusing on providing services to insurance companies and utilizing its databases to assist drug companies in patient identification for clinical trials. By diversifying its services, Waterdrop aims to navigate the changing landscape and continue its growth trajectory.
Transactions in DFDS shares by managers and closely related parties reported for public disclosure.

data.title: Eightcap partners with Orbital to enhance global trading with stablecoin payments in 120+ countries.
data.description: Eightcap has established stablecoins as the cornerstone of its worldwide trading system through a recent partnership. This collaboration aims to enhance funding and settlement processes across 120+ nations. The development, based in London, Tallinn, Estonia, and Gibraltar, was announced on December 5, 2025. Orbital, the platform facilitating cross-border transactions, will support this initiative.

data.title: Paranovus Entertainment Technology Limited sees 18,037% YoY revenue growth in 2025 interim results.
data.description: On December 5, 2025, Paranovus Entertainment Technology Limited, a Nasdaq-listed company, disclosed its financial outcomes for the six months concluding on September 30, 2025. The Company experienced a notable rise in revenue and a significant enhancement in gross profit during this period.

data.title: Owlet launches Dream Sock™ in Australia & New Zealand after TGA approval, expanding global reach for pediatric health monitoring.
data.description: In Sydney, Owlet, Inc. (NYSE: OWLT), a leading company in smart infant monitoring, has introduced Dream Sock™ for families in Australia and New Zealand. This product has been approved by the Australian Therapeutic Goods Administration (TGA), ensuring it meets the high medical device standards in the area. Families can now buy Dream Sock through Owlet’s website and some retail partners. This launch marks a significant step forward for Owlet in the region, offering families a new option to monitor their infants.

data.title: Bitcoin whales are amassing, with $5.7B realized losses and $5.8B capitulation losses, suggesting a bullish shift.
data.description: Large holders of Bitcoin, also known as "whales," are rapidly acquiring the cryptocurrency as losses from market capitulation amount to $5.8 billion. This surge in accumulation suggests a possible turnaround towards a bullish trend in the market.

data.title: Study finds outpatient 360° Lumbar Fusion is safe, with 100% discharge within 23 hours and up to 60% cost savings compared to hospitals.
data.description: A recent study conducted at DISC Surgery Centers in Newport Beach, California, analyzed 203 patients and found that all patients were discharged within 23 hours. The study also revealed that the total episode-of-care costs for fusion procedures at these centers were up to 60% lower compared to hospital-based procedures. The research was led by orthopedic spine surgeon Steven J. and provides promising insights into more efficient and cost-effective healthcare options for patients undergoing fusion procedures.

data.title: Atomic launches PayNow to simplify recurring bill payments, expanding product suite to bring clarity and convenience.
data.description: In Salt Lake City, a press release dated October 30, 2025, announced the launch of "PayNow" by Atomic. The product is designed to simplify recurring bill payments and aims to bring clarity, confidence, and convenience to paying bills within banking apps. Atomic, a prominent player in embedded financial connectivity, expanded its product suite with the introduction of PayNow. This initiative is part of the company's efforts to transition from complexity to control. The updated release emphasizes the focus on providing a seamless experience for users managing recurring payments through the PayNow platform.

data.title: Home experts warn homeowners about winter siding damage, emphasizing prevention of cracks, moisture intrusion, and cold-weather deterioration.
data.description: Experts from Ideal Siding in Vancouver, British Columbia discussed effective strategies to prevent cracks, moisture intrusion, and cold-weather deterioration in homes. With winter storms and freezing temperatures affecting North America, experts emphasize the importance of safeguarding properties against potential damage. Cold weather conditions can lead to serious issues such as cracks and moisture seepage, compromising the structural integrity of buildings. By implementing proper insulation, sealing gaps, and conducting regular maintenance, homeowners can protect their properties against the harsh winter elements. The experts also highlight the significance of addressing any existing damages promptly to avoid further deterioration. As temperatures drop, ensuring that homes are properly maintained becomes crucial to prevent costly repairs in the future.

data.title: Dow Jones rises before inflation data, while AI stock drops on poor sales. Live updates on Market Today.
data.description: The stock market saw positive movement today as the Dow Jones index experienced a rise before the release of crucial inflation data. Conversely, an artificial intelligence (AI) company's stock took a hit due to disappointing sales figures. This event was covered in real-time by Investor's Business Daily. The Dow Jones index witnessed an increase, while the AI stock faced a decline in response to its earnings report.

data.title: Proficient hodlers strategically use disciplined DCA and rules-based systems to buy crypto dips, avoiding impulsive decisions.
data.description: Experienced investors do not react impulsively to market fluctuations; instead, they establish predetermined investment proportions, rely on automated systems to make purchases based on specific criteria, and implement Dollar Cost Averaging strategies as part of a methodical, analytics-driven approach to decision-making.

data.title: FDA approves Bristol Myers' Breyanzi for relapsed MZL, marking its 5th approval for different cancers, becoming top CAR T therapy.
data.description: Yesterday, the FDA gave the green light to Bristol Myers Squibb & Co.'s Breyanzi (lisocabtagene maraleucel; liso-cel), a CD19-directed chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T cell therapy, for adult patients with relapsed or refractory marginal zone lymphoma (MZL). This one-time infusion treatment is now approved for this condition, making Breyanzi the sole FDA-approved CAR T cell therapy for five cancer types, the most among CD19-directed CAR T cell therapies. For more details, check out the complete article on Benzinga.com.
